## [00:00 - 03:01]

Okay, so when I first started in business, I started publishing about the stock market. That was something that I had some expertise in. I had studied. I spent a lot of time studying the stock market, but I didn't have any credentials. I didn't have any letters after my name. I wasn't a stockbroker. I wasn't a registered investment advisor. I didn't have any degrees in the stock market. It was just something I had studied a lot about. And frankly, I was desperate to start a business. I'd learned about this whole idea of selling information online. And so I wanted to try it. It was something I knew about, so I started publishing a newsletter. In truth be told, I didn't really have the wherewithal to sell something. I didn't know how to sell stuff. I didn't have the self-confidence to start selling something since I didn't have any letters after my name, anything like that. So I just started publishing a newsletter. In the very first newsletter, I sent out to everyone, all my friends and family who had an email address. There were 19 email addresses for that first newsletter. Actually one of those was my second email address. One was my wife's email address. So outside of my immediate family, 17 people got that newsletter. And then I gradually built that up from there and it grew through word of mouth. I kept on publishing this newsletter and eventually I came out with paid versions of the newsletter, but I always kept that free version of the newsletter. And at that time, I had this friend, this acquaintance, and he was in that business. And he had been in that business of publishing stock market newsletters for a long time. In fact, his dad was in the business and that's how he came into the business. And I remember him telling me that he couldn't figure out why I was publishing this free newsletter. It was the best quality newsletter I could put out at the time. And it became pretty darn popular and actually grew through word of mouth. But he just thought it was insane. I was publishing this newsletter for free. He couldn't figure it out. He was charging $1,000 a month for his newsletter. And so he's like, why would you do this? He had this long history of publishing. This is before the internet. It went way back and then his dad's newsletter went back and he couldn't figure it out. But a couple years later, he's like, yeah, I used to think you were crazy, but it was sort of like crazy like a fox because he could see I had built up this big platform. I built up this big list. And I did it. And in fact, he came around asking to partner with me to try to get to my expertise. And it was just because of the strategy I had developed.
